description: Manages cryptographic key material for end-to-end encrypted messaging. Handles device key publication, one-time pre-key upload/claiming, and cross-server key queries.

actors.name: Sending Device
actors.description: Device establishing an encrypted session with recipients

actors.name: Recipient Device
actors.description: Device whose keys are being queried to set up encryption

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Key distribution server; stores and serves keys without decrypting content

fields.name: device_id
fields.label: Identifier of the device the keys belong to

fields.name: identity_keys
fields.label: Long-lived public keys for the device (curve25519 and ed25519)

fields.name: one_time_keys
fields.label: Single-use pre-keys uploaded in bulk to bootstrap Olm sessions

fields.name: fallback_keys
fields.label: Fallback pre-keys used when no one-time keys remain

fields.name: claimed_keys
fields.label: One-time keys retrieved by another device to initiate encryption

fields.name: failures
fields.label: Map of server domains to error descriptions for failed queries

key_management: The server stores device keys and one-time keys but never stores private key material
One-time keys are consumed exactly once; after claiming they are removed from the pool
When one-time keys are exhausted, fallback keys are used and marked for replacement
Devices that are deleted have their associated keys removed from the store
querying: Device key queries must be separated by domain: local devices from database, remote via federation
Remote device key results are cached to reduce federation load; stale cache entries refreshed on demand
Servers that are unreachable during a query are recorded in the failures response
Servers previously marked as down are not contacted until their backoff window expires

given: uploaded keys include valid signatures from the device

then.description: Device identity keys stored and indexed for querying

device_keys_uploaded.result: Device keys are discoverable by other users in shared rooms

given: uploaded one-time keys have valid signatures

then.description: One-time keys added to the available pool for this device

one_time_keys_uploaded.result: Other devices can claim these keys to establish Olm sessions

given: requester specifies one or more target devices
target device has at least one unclaimed one-time key

then.description: Claimed one-time key removed from pool; cannot be reused

one_time_key_claimed.result: Requester can use the claimed key to establish an encrypted Olm session

given: target device has no unclaimed one-time keys
fallback_key_used.result: Encryption session established using fallback key; device should upload new one-time keys

given: queried users are all on the local server
keys_queried_local.result: Device keys returned from local database with cross-signing bundles

given: queried users include users on remote servers
keys_queried_federated.result: Keys for remote users returned; unreachable servers listed in failures

given: one or more remote servers are unreachable or in backoff

then.description: Unreachable servers recorded in failures map

key_query_partial_failure.result: Available keys returned; caller can retry for failed servers later

given: target device has no one-time keys and no fallback key
no_keys_available.result: No key returned for that device; encryption session cannot be established

errors.message: No one-time keys available for this device
Key query could not be completed for one or more servers

events.name: keys.device_keys_uploaded
events.description: A device's long-lived identity keys were published

events.name: keys.one_time_keys_uploaded
events.description: A batch of one-time pre-keys was added to a device's pool

events.name: keys.one_time_key_claimed
events.description: A one-time key was consumed to bootstrap an Olm session

events.name: keys.fallback_key_used
events.description: A fallback key was used because no one-time keys remained

events.name: keys.query_completed
events.description: A device key query was resolved (fully or partially)

events.name: keys.query_partial_failure
events.description: Key query completed but one or more servers were unreachable

related.reason: Cross-signing keys are bundled in key query responses alongside device keys
Device keys are invalidated when the device is deleted
Session keys established during key exchange are backed up for recovery
Remote device keys are fetched via federation queries

goals.description: Manages cryptographic key material for end-to-end encrypted messaging. Handles device key publication, one-time pre-key upload/claiming, and cross-server key queries.

constraints.description: Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

tradeoffs.reason: authentication must prioritize preventing unauthorized access

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
